source_truth.primary_source: U.S. EPA Office of Water — WATERS Services REST API
source_truth.geofabric: NHDPlus V2.1 (Puerto Rico = VPU 21; Vogel/VPUAttribute extensions unavailable -> attribute_coverage stamped partial)

real_data_sources.aee_incidents: SuperSonicHub1/luma-energy-outages outages_by_town.json (CC0; modern per-municipio realization of the legacy AEEIncidents town/area model after PREPA->LUMA 2021). NOTE: that mirror is itself inactive since 2025-03-03; committed data is a reproducible point-in-time snapshot at tier T2/needs_review. Wire a LIVE source (PR Energy Bureau regulatory outage reports / MiLUMA with permission) for ongoing attribution — the adapter (scripts/ingest_aee.py) is source-agnostic.
real_data_sources.pr_municipios_geo: data/geo/pr_municipios.json (Census Gazetteer centroids: normalization + spatial joins) + data/geo/pr_municipios.geojson (78 municipio polygons) + data/geo/pr_barrios.geojson (901 barrios, each linked to its municipio) for the map layer; U.S. Census cartographic boundaries (public domain), WGS84

hub_callable_commands.setup: [ -d ../thehub-pr ] || git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/jotaele44/thehub-pr.git ../thehub-pr; python -m pip install uv && uv pip install --system -e .[dev]
hub_callable_commands.fetch_luma_live: python3 scripts/fetch_luma_live.py --out /tmp/outages_by_town.json  (LIVE MiLUMA API; ToS-gated, see script header)

resolved_conditions: real-data load complete: POWER assets (39 generation/substation/transmission nodes via scripts/ingest_power.py) and WATER/wastewater assets (234 treatment/wastewater/pumping/reservoir nodes via scripts/ingest_water.py, OSM-derived, review_status=needs_review) are loaded from real public data — 273 utility_assets total

advisory: PREPS service_events remain island-wide aggregates; per-municipality outage attribution is now provided by aee_incidents (LUMA outages_by_town) at tier T2/needs_review (third-party snapshot) — promote to T1 if/when a direct utility feed is wired

advisory (provenance): aee_incidents is a point-in-time snapshot (2025-03-03); the recommended SuperSonicHub1 mirror is itself inactive since then and the payload carries no timestamps, so a LIVE per-municipio source is required for ongoing attribution; outage start/end + restoration events require a commit-walk + snapshot-diff (future extension)
advisory: source datasets (Energy_Sector, preps_*, outages_by_town.json, Census gazetteer) live outside the repo; ingest scripts default to local paths (override with --src)

water-monitoring analytic layer built: scripts/build_water_alerts.py promotes real EPA SDWIS boil-water + health-based quality violations to CONTAMINATION AlertEvents (T1), plus a T2/needs_review reservoir-low HYDRO_OPS proxy; scripts/build_water_power_crosswalk.py adds water->power `energizes` edges (closes GAP-003); the WATERS NHDPlus enricher is wired (scripts/enrich_waters_nhd.py). Rich asset attributes + energized_by relationships now flow to thehub-pr. Proxies are transparently tier-tagged; T1 unblocks (AAA operating levels, utility feeder map, WATERS key) recorded in docs/ROAD_TO_100.md.

centinelas_pr_only_gaps: PREB (Puerto Rico Energy Bureau) regulatory filings: document-based portal, no structured API — centinelas-pr monitors filings and sends signals via ingest_news_event.py until PREB publishes a machine-readable feed

AAA/PRASA boil-water notices: the AAA portal is a Webflow marketing site with no public API — SDWIS T1 microbial violations (ingest_sdwis_violations.py) are the authoritative regulatory boil-water signal; individual AAA press-release notices route through centinelas-pr -> ingest_news_event.py at T3
waf_blocked_sources: MiLUMA / api.miluma.lumapr.com: Incapsula WAF returns HTTP 403 to plain clients; fetch_luma_live.py attempts a browser User-Agent workaround but success is not guaranteed; activate via refresh.py --all (optional step, warns and continues on WAF failure); a formal PREB/LUMA data-sharing arrangement is the recommended path to T1 electric outage attribution
